evidence_items.text: Startup loads or initializes a persisted local runtime workspace under `.jeff_runtime`. On a fresh or reset runtime, Jeff initializes `project-1` / `wu-1` with no seeded run history; later startups reload the persisted canonical state and support records already present in that workspace. No daemon, GUI, or background continuation is started.

findings.text: Jeff initializes a local runtime workspace under .jeff_runtime, creating project-1 / wu-1 without seeded run history on fresh or reset runtimes, and reloading persisted state and records on subsequent startups.
The system does not start a GUI, daemon, or background continuation.
Jeff's primary strengths include truthful inspection, bounded /run, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage.
Jeff is not designed as a broad API surface, a broad memo, an autonomous continuation system, or a GUI.

inferences: Jeff relies on local file system persistence to maintain state between sessions.
question: startup summary

source_items.snippet: # Jeff Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone. It is strongest today at truthful inspection, bounded `/run`, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage. It is not a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memo
source_items.title: README.md

summary: Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone that initializes or loads a local runtime workspace located under .jeff_runtime. It focuses on truthful inspection, research support, and anti-drift coverage, rather than serving as a GUI or autonomous system.
uncertainties: No explicit uncertainties identified from the provided evidence.
